narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout
narratives.thesis: The demand for AI infrastructure, particularly high-performance GPUs and specialized chips, continues to drive significant capital expenditure and revenue growth for key semiconductor players.
watchFor: Hyperscaler capex cuts at next earnings
NVDA earnings miss or weak guidance
New competitive threats from custom silicon

narratives.name: GLP-1 Obesity Wave
narratives.thesis: The widespread adoption and expanding therapeutic applications of GLP-1 drugs for obesity and related conditions are driving significant growth and market re-ratings for pharmaceutical companies at the forefront of this trend.
watchFor: New, more effective competitive drugs emerge
Insurance coverage restrictions tighten significantly
Manufacturing capacity constraints limit supply

narratives.name: AI Software & Cloud Adoption
narratives.thesis: Enterprises are increasingly adopting AI-powered software and cloud solutions, driving growth for companies providing AI development platforms, security, and integration services.
watchFor: Enterprise IT spending slowdown
Increased regulatory scrutiny on AI data usage
Significant pricing pressure in cloud services

narratives.name: Semiconductor Equipment Expansion
narratives.thesis: The ongoing buildout of advanced semiconductor manufacturing capacity, driven by AI and other high-performance computing needs, is creating strong demand for semiconductor equipment and materials.

Geopolitical tensions impacting equipment sales to China
Slower-than-expected ramp of new fabs

narratives.name: AI Data Center Power Demand
narratives.thesis: The exponential growth of AI data centers is creating unprecedented demand for electricity, directly benefiting independent power producers capable of supplying reliable and scalable energy solutions.

Government policies shift away from supporting new power generation
Technological advancements drastically reduce data center power consumption

narratives.name: AI Security Demand
narratives.thesis: The proliferation of AI systems and data creates new security vulnerabilities, driving increased demand for advanced AI-native cybersecurity solutions.
watchFor: Major AI security breach that erodes confidence in current solutions
Consolidation in the cybersecurity market reducing competition
Shift to open-source or platform-native security tools

narratives.name: Digital Ad & Content Dominance
narratives.thesis: Major internet content and information platforms continue to dominate digital advertising and content consumption, leveraging AI to enhance user engagement and monetization.
watchFor: Antitrust actions against major platforms
User exodus to alternative platforms
Significant data privacy regulations impacting ad targeting

narratives.name: Memory Trade Reawakening
narratives.thesis: The memory market is showing signs of a reawakening, driven by potential supply disruptions and increasing demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) used in AI applications, leading to price target increases and stock rallies.
watchFor: Resolution of Samsung strike impacting NAND supply
Oversupply concerns re-emerge in the broader memory market
Slower-than-expected HBM adoption in AI servers

narratives.thesis: E-commerce platforms continue to show growth, with companies expanding their reach and integrating new technologies like AI to enhance customer experience and operational efficiency.

Increased regulatory pressure on large e-commerce platforms
New competitive threats from social commerce platforms

narratives.thesis: Credit services companies are demonstrating strength through strategic partnerships, expanded offerings, and the integration of AI to enhance payment processing and fraud detection.

narratives.name: Logistics Operational Headwinds
narratives.thesis: The integrated freight and logistics sector faces operational headwinds from rising fuel costs and potential labor disputes, impacting profitability despite strong demand in some areas.

Technology.thesis: The Technology sector remains broadly bullish, primarily driven by the relentless expansion of the AI capex cycle across semiconductors, software, and cloud infrastructure. While some sub-sectors are seeing profit-taking, the underlying demand for AI-related hardware and software continues to fuel growth.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing, killing duration bid for software multiples
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced AI chips — significant supply chain disruption

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: The Consumer Cyclical sector is mixed, with some areas showing resilience due to strong consumer spending and AI-driven growth narratives, while others face headwinds from rising gas prices and inflation concerns impacting discretionary spending.
watchFor: CPI for all items rises > 0.6% MoM — further erodes consumer purchasing power
Gasoline prices continue to rise, impacting discretionary spending
Retail sales data shows significant deceleration

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is mixed, with digital content and information platforms continuing to innovate, particularly with AI, but traditional entertainment facing challenges and evolving consumer preferences.
watchFor: Major tech companies pull back on digital ad spending
New regulatory frameworks impacting content platforms
Significant shift in consumer streaming habits

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is mixed, with Aerospace & Defense benefiting from geopolitical tensions, while other sub-industries face varying demand signals and operational challenges.
watchFor: De-escalation of major geopolitical conflicts
Significant cuts to defense budgets
Global trade slowdown impacting logistics and machinery demand

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is bullish, primarily driven by the transformative impact of GLP-1 obesity drugs and ongoing innovation in medical devices, despite some policy and cost pressures.
watchFor: Unexpected safety concerns or side effects for GLP-1 drugs
Aggressive government price controls on pharmaceuticals
Major healthcare policy changes impacting insurance coverage

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is mixed, with some banks and credit services firms benefiting from strategic initiatives and AI adoption, while others navigate broader market volatility and regulatory scrutiny.
watchFor: Federal Reserve raises interest rates unexpectedly
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit card delinquencies
New financial regulations impacting profitability

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector is stable, with discount stores benefiting from consumers seeking value amidst inflation, while household and personal products companies focus on strategic brand management.
watchFor: Significant economic recession leading to widespread job losses
Deflationary pressures impacting pricing power
Major shifts in consumer brand loyalty

Utilities.thesis: The Utilities sector is experiencing a bullish tailwind from the surging demand for power driven by AI data centers, particularly benefiting independent power producers and those investing in renewable energy solutions.
watchFor: Slowdown in AI data center buildout
Regulatory hurdles for new power generation projects
Significant decline in energy prices

Basic Materials.thesis: The Basic Materials sector, specifically industrial metals and mining, is facing headwinds from specific company-level challenges and market dynamics, despite broader interest in rare earth elements.
watchFor: Significant increase in global industrial demand
New government policies supporting domestic mining
Breakthroughs in rare earth extraction technologies

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are in a neutral stance, with gold and silver experiencing mixed signals driven by inflation concerns, geopolitical developments, and broader market sentiment, rather than a clear directional trend.
watchFor: CPI for all items rises > 0.6% MoM — reignites inflation hedge demand
Significant escalation of geopolitical conflicts
Sharp decline in the US dollar index
